They cleaned it every day,

When you think of North Korea, you might
think of this, but probably not this.

It's a new seaside resort
that opened in July,

but so far, the only foreign tourists
who have been allowed to visit

are a group of Russians.
And you might be curious why.

Well, that is what
we're going to be finding out today.

I'm Hannah Gelbart. This is What in
the World from the BBC World Service

and I'm speaking to Rachel Lee, a
BBC reporter in Seoul in South Korea.
Hey, Rachel. Hello.

So North Korea has opened up
this new beach resort Wonsan Kalma.

It's just a few hours drive away
from the capital,

from Pyongyang.
What's it actually like?

So the Wonsan Kalma resort is
on North Korea's east coast,
and it's in Kangwon Province.

Now, what's interesting is that this
is actually a spot for Kim family,

and Kim family is basically
the three generations who've been
ruling the dynasty of North Korea,

and they have been using this spot
for decades as a private retreat.
And the location is fascinating.

You've got these sandy beaches
looking out over the East Sea,
and there are green hills behind it,

mountains that link up to
the well-known mountain Kumgang area.

And the resort complex
is huge as well.

So over 7000 rooms spread
across these hotels,

private seafront villas
and mid-range accommodations.

And another thing is there's also
a water park with big slides,

sports stadiums, theatres
and shopping arcades as well.

And I read that it also used to be
a missile testing site too.
It is actually, it is.

But what's interesting is that they're
trying to change the image of the region
and the image of North Korea overall.

So this is you know,
they're just trying to say this is -

that North Korea is not just
about missiles or military parades.

And we can also be
a luxury travel destinations, too,

if you can afford it
and play by our rules.

It's so interesting that they want
to be a luxury tourist destination

because North Korea is -
it's so closed and it's so secretive.

And for years, hardly any foreign
tourists have been allowed in at all.

And now the only people who've been
able to visit this resort are

a small group of Russian tourists.
And they went in July.

Why is it that only these Russian
tourists were allowed in?

I think that is because Pyongyang
has kept most foreign tourism

closed since the Covid 19 pandemic
border shutdown in early 2020

and reopening
has been extremely selective,

and Russia is one of the few countries
with which North Korea's relationship

has warmed dramatically
in the last couple of years.

And some of you might have heard
a lot about, you know,

their shared opposition to the West
and also growing military cooperation

between the two and the Russians
who did visit Wonsan,

they have arrived on package deals
costing around 1800 USD.

That's about £1340,
and that is

more than 1.5 times the average
monthly rate wage in Russia.
So not exactly a mass market price.

No, but it does really sound
like a premium package holiday.

And the BBC actually spoke to one of
the Russian tourists who went there.

This is Anastasia Samsonova.
We woke up and went to breakfast,

but those who didn't want to go
didn't have to go.

And then after breakfast,
within 15 minutes we would get ready

They came to the beach

When we arrived, the first thing we

They cleaned it every day,
making sure it was smooth and

that everything was perfectly neat.

Honestly, everything was new and the

It really was a fantastic beach.

was the North Korean

We mentioned, for example,

but it seems
like they didn't allow swimming.

That's why I don't believe there's

Rachel, we have talked a little bit

I'm sure a lot of people

to be curious about that, too.
Wonsan missile testing site is,

as I said, it's the north of the city of

and this is often called

Now it's one of the North Korea's
most active missile launch locations,

And Kim Jong UN has

Point about classified areas,

And I know it's not common

but do you think when they do, they
can usually wander around freely?

Or was this a special case?
Well, I think it's a special case.

I mean, she said she could wander

but that - I mean, that may be true,

but this is only true
for this particular group.

But it's most important to stress
that how unusual this is.

So North Korea is one of the
most controlled tourism environments

And normally visitors are assigned

And in this case,

And why do you think they relaxed

It's like a testing group.

So as I said, Russia is a very friendly

They're one of the closest allies.

And with these - all the military

Kim Jong Un wants to earn this

without any international sanctions.
And tourism is one of the ways.

So they're trying to show

As you say,
it is a totalitarian state.

It's one of the most

like communication tools -

True. So the Wonsan Kalma project has

Demanding near constant work

Well, North Korea hasn't really

why if North Korea did open its doors

there would be controversy

that North Korea, I'm pretty sure

So for now, only this group

Are there any signs

that they're trying to attract a lot

North Korea, the tension
